summary: A superb matrix capture of a 1970 monster show – the Dark Star is other‑worldly while St. Stephen / Not Fade Away / Lovelight flow in transcendent jams.
review: Listeners rave about this recording’s crystal‑clear matrix mix that puts you right in the hall, and they can’t stop praising the performance – a blazing Dark Star, soaring St. Stephen into Not Fade Away and Turn On Your Lovelight are repeatedly called “astonishing”.  The band is described as being completely in the zone with Jerry’s tone, Bob’s chord work and Pigpen’s wild vocals all captured in an immersive soundstage.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews (most 5‑star) uniformly praise both the musical performance and the matrix recording quality; only a few low‑star comments address track‑break annoyance, not the show itself.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers highlight a spacious, ‘hall‑effect’ mix with crystal‑clear instrument separation and vivid ambience.  Minor complaints focus on track‑break handling rather than audio fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as a seamless, exploratory journey with flawless transitions (Dark Star → St Stephen → Not Fade Away → Lovelight) that keep the crowd in ecstatic motion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly called angelic, other‑worldly and “Vox‑wah”‑enhanced; his improvisational depth drives the Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (referred to as Bobby) gets praise for building chords and adding vocal energy, especially during the jams.
